commit.message:
Audit fixes: base class, TV names, error UX, saved settings, grammar

- Extract StatefulTvAction base class: toggle-power, power-on, and
  turn-off shared identical onWillAppear/onWillDisappear logic; now
  they all extend a single base class eliminating the duplication.

- TV scanner now fetches the UPnP device description XML from the
  LOCATION header to get each TV's configured friendly name (e.g.
  "Living Room TV"), falling back to "LG TV" if the fetch fails or
  no LOCATION header is present.

- plugin.ts sends error: "not_connected" in inputList/appList responses
  when the TV is not connected, so the PI can show a clear message
  instead of "No inputs found."

- set-input.html and launch-app.html now subscribe to didReceiveSettings
  to pre-populate the select with the saved value when the PI opens.

- Fix manifest.json description grammar/capitalisation.

- Move vitest pool config to vitest.config.ts with maxForks=4 to
  reliably prevent the ESM module cache race under --pool=forks.

commit.message:
fix: consume pendingScrapeUrl post-signup, delete Clerk user on account deletion (#148, #152)

- UrlInputCta: redirect to /sign-up with redirect_url encoding the import URL so Clerk forwards user to /dashboard?import=<url> after signup; localStorage write kept as fallback
- dashboard/page: add useEffect on mount that reads pendingScrapeUrl from localStorage, removes it, and pushes to /products?importUrl=<url>
- products/page: add useSearchParams; useEffect reads importUrl query param, pre-fills importUrl state, opens the import dialog, and auto-selects the first brand if one exists

Wire AI insights into film card — Tonight's watch + Discovery

  data["aiInsights"] (correct camelCase key) instead of the broken
  "ai_insights" snake_case that always returned empty

  film_primary and film_profile with AI-suggested pick

  requiring a trailing (year)

  discovery standalone

film intelligence. Root cause was key name mismatch — data stores
ai_insights as "aiInsights" at line 12116 but override was reading
"ai_insights" → always empty dict.

commit.message:
Preserve original language/script for person names in LLM prompt

The system prompt example romanized 张三 → Zhang San, causing the LLM
to transliterate all Chinese names. Fix by keeping names in their
original script and adding explicit instructions to never romanize.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix excelParser: add dayOfWeek forward-fill + robust parity parsing

Critical bug: when Excel has merged cells for day of week column,
only the first row got the correct day — all subsequent rows defaulted
to Monday (dayOfWeek=1). This caused magistratura schedules to show
only Saturday (the last parsed day) while other lessons were assigned
wrong days and filtered out by parity.

Fixes:
- Added forward-fill for dayOfWeek (lastDayOfWeek variable)
- Added parseParity() function handling text values (нч/ч/нечёт/чёт)
- Added abbreviated day names (ПН, ВТ, СР, ЧТ, ПТ, СБ, ВС)

commit.message:
fix: strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from spawn env to prevent overriding claude's auth

Worker was passing process.env (including placeholder ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...)
to the spawned claude process, overriding its own stored credentials and causing
"Invalid API key" / exit code 1 on every task run.

Fix: destructure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY out before passing env to spawn().
Also: clear placeholder from .env.example and clarify comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: handle notification tap on cold start (app opened from killed state)

Previously, tapping a notification while the app was closed would start the
app but never navigate — the response listener wasn't attached yet when the
tap fired (isAuthenticated was still false during session restore).

Fix: call getLastNotificationResponseAsync() once after authentication
resolves to catch the cold-start tap, while keeping the existing listener
for foreground/background taps. Extracted navigateFromNotification() to
share logic between the two paths.

Co-authored-by: Pablo <pabloguege@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set nat_flags in XDP conntrack fast-path for IPv6 SNAT

handle_ct_hit_v4() and handle_ct_hit_v6() in xdp_conntrack.c set
meta->src_ip from the session's NAT addresses and route to XDP_PROG_NAT,
but never set meta->nat_flags.  nat_rewrite_v6() gates the source address
rewrite on (meta->nat_flags & SESS_FLAG_SNAT), so without this flag the
IPv6 source address was never rewritten on the conntrack fast-path.

IPv4 SNAT was silently unaffected because nat_rewrite_v4() compares
meta->src_ip.v4 != iph->saddr directly without checking nat_flags.

TC conntrack (tc_conntrack.c) already had the correct
meta->nat_flags = sess->flags & (SESS_FLAG_SNAT | SESS_FLAG_DNAT)
assignment — XDP was simply missing it.

Also cleans up debug trace code from bpfrx_nat.h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace CRF spinbox with friendly video quality preset picker

CRF (0-51, lower = better) is unintuitive for non-technical users.
Replace with a named ComboBox mapping to sensible CRF values:
  Efficient       → CRF 28  (smallest file)
  Balanced        → CRF 23  (recommended, h264 default)
  High quality    → CRF 18  (visually lossless)
  Maximum         → CRF 12  (largest file)

Config still stores video_crf as a raw value for compatibility.
Default preset is Balanced (CRF 23).

feat: add transcript dedup + os.Logger logging

Speaker bleed fix: TranscriptViewModel now suppresses host chunks that
are >=75% similar (Levenshtein) to a recent guest chunk within 5 seconds.
This catches mic pickup of speaker audio that passes the RMS gate.

Added structured logging via os.Logger (viewable in Console.app, filter
by "com.nmonic"):
- Log.audio: mic RMS levels, system audio capture, device info
- Log.transcription: Deepgram connection, chunks, dedup suppressions
- Replaced all print() calls with appropriate log levels

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: invoke skills as slash commands and close stdin to prevent hanging

Two fixes for headless skill execution:

1. Invoke skills as /skill-name (same as terminal) instead of pasting raw
   content. Claude resolves the skill from ~/.claude/skills/ natively,
   giving it full context including sub-skills and the skills system.

2. Set stdio: ['ignore','pipe','pipe'] on spawn. Without this, claude sees
   an open stdin pipe and waits for input indefinitely — causing the 3+min
   hang. 'ignore' closes stdin immediately.

Also adds 5-min timeout (configurable via TASK_TIMEOUT_MS env var) to kill
any task that still hangs and mark it failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix reliability bugs in main.py

- Avoid double r.json() parse in github_download_file
- Use params= instead of data= for GET request in get_updates
- Add timeout=30 to all requests calls to prevent thread hangs
- Parallelize account file fetching in parse_accounts with ThreadPoolExecutor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix intermittent test failures by using --pool=forks

Vitest's default threads pool has a module caching conflict with ESM
dynamic imports (await import()) causing all suites to report "No test
suite found" when run together. Forks pool isolates each file properly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add output resolution downscale option to timelapse

Add 'Output resolution' setting (Original / 1920px / 1440px / 1280px / 720px)
defaulting to 1920px. When set, ffmpeg scales the longest side down before
encoding, dramatically reducing file size with no perceptible quality loss
for timelapse playback.

At 2628x2628 with CRF 10 the encoder was producing 666 Mbps / 1.16 GB for
a 14-second clip. At 1920px + CRF 23 the same session should be ~30-60 MB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add project skeleton and configuration

Go module with go-nostr v0.52.3 and YARA-X v1.13.0 (CGO).
Makefile with CGO_ENABLED=1. Config loads from env vars with
.env fallback; deduplicates relay URLs to work around a
go-nostr SimplePool bug where duplicates prevent channel close.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zss
repository.description: Zapstore Scanner Service — ClamAV + YARA-X malware scanning for Android APKs on Nostr

commit.message:
Add APK download and extraction

Download: streams from Blossom CDN with SHA256 verification,
500MB size limit, 3 retries with backoff, TLS 1.2 minimum.

Extraction: zip handling with security protections:
- Zip-slip: path validation against destination prefix
- Zip bomb: 2GB total extraction limit
- Symlinks: skipped entirely
- Hidden DEX detection: walks all files checking for dex\n
  magic bytes to catch payloads disguised as data files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zss
repository.description: Zapstore Scanner Service — ClamAV + YARA-X malware scanning for Android APKs on Nostr

commit.message:
Add Android YARA rules

15 rules across 5 categories: permissions (SMS, calls, camera,
device admin, accessibility), network (suspicious URLs, dynamic
code loading, runtime exec), data exfiltration (contacts,
location, clipboard), obfuscation (known packers), and crypto
(BIP39 seed theft, clipboard address swapping).

Only the 2 crypto rules are critical severity (trigger malware
verdict). All others are informational — reported in scan
results but do not affect the pass/fail verdict.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zss
repository.description: Zapstore Scanner Service — ClamAV + YARA-X malware scanning for Android APKs on Nostr

commit.message:
Add CLI scan mode and relay daemon

CLI: "zss scan <apk>" runs ClamAV + YARA concurrently, prints
JSON report, exits 0 (clean) or 1 (malware). --publish flag
signs and publishes kind:1985 label to configured relays.

Daemon: "zss daemon" subscribes to kind:3063 + kind:1063 via
SimplePool.SubscribeManyNotifyEOSE. EOSE-aware processing:
rate-limited during historical backfill, immediate for live
events. Worker pool downloads, scans, and publishes labels.
Watermark persisted on completion for reliable restart.
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zss
repository.description: Zapstore Scanner Service — ClamAV + YARA-X malware scanning for Android APKs on Nostr

commit.message:
Add ClamAV and YARA-X scanner engines

ClamAV: z-prefix unix socket protocol (zSCAN), parses response
by last ": " separator to handle macOS symlink resolution,
reads only first null-terminated segment. 180s timeout.

YARA-X: compiles all .yar files at startup, per-scan Scanner
with Destroy(), extracts rule metadata (severity, category).
200MB per-file limit. Tested at 271 MB/s throughput.

Verdict is binary clean/malware. Only ClamAV infections and
critical-severity YARA rules (crypto seed theft, address swap)
trigger malware. All matches reported for transparency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: zss
repository.description: Zapstore Scanner Service — ClamAV + YARA-X malware scanning for Android APKs on Nostr

commit.message:
Add Nostr integration and persistence store

Nostr: NsecSigner (adapted from zsp), per-relay connect/publish/
close with 30s timeout. Builds NIP-32 label events (kind:1985)
with com.zapstore.security namespace and NIP-09 deletion events
(kind:5) for re-scan updates.

Store: two-tier JSONL persistence —
- Scan cache (SHA256 → ScanReport): avoids re-scanning identical
  bytes when multiple events reference the same APK
- Label store (event ID → label event ID): one label per asset
  event, enables NIP-09 deletion on re-scan
- Watermark: advanced only after successful scan+publish+persist,
  so failed jobs are retried on restart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
